title: Elastic Rebound
definition: The theory that earthquakes occur due to the release of stored elastic energy when tectonic plates slip along faults, as described by the theory of elastic rebound.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that earthquakes are caused by the sudden release of built-up tension in Earth's crust along fault lines, leading to seismic waves.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The principle that the buildup and subsequent release of elastic energy along fault zones cause earthquakes as explained by the theory of elastic rebound.
difficulty_levels.expert: The concept that stress builds up along locked faults, eventually leading to a catastrophic release of stored elastic energy and causing seismic waves, as described by the theory of elastic rebound.

extra_fields.insight: The buildup and sudden release of elastic energy in Earth's crust as the cause of earthquakes.
